creator: iwtcex@gmail.com
text:
(In reply to Stefan Eßer from comment #4)

> there used to be a separate port

We got rid of i386-wine because it was even more painful for WoW64 due to i386-wine and normal wine packages being _always_ out of sync.

> Locally building the i386 version on amd64 is possible with poudriere in an i386 jail.

Or in chroot: https://wiki.freebsd.org/i386-Wine#Manual. Don't mind i386-wine instructions there, it's exactly the same procedure. `make buildworld` is also unnecessary vs downloading base tarballs (see misc/freebsd-release-manifests for checksums).
